zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Melton 7th Lord Lucy於1398年出生在英國德比夏爾時，他的父親Thomas Melton31歲，母親Lady Margaret Clifford31歲。他於大約1421年與Elizabeth Hilton在英國英格蘭德比夏爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1455年在英國德比夏爾享年57歲。

nameMeanings.name: Melton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places, for example in Leicestershire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, and Yorkshire, all of which have the same origin as Middleton , with Old English middel replaced by its Old Norse equivalent methal after the Scandinavian settlement of northern and eastern England.

variants.name: Middleton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places so called. In over thirty instances from many different areas, the name is from Old English midel ‘middle’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. However, Middleton on the Hill near Leominster in Herefordshire appears in Domesday Book as Miceltune, the first element clearly being Old English micel ‘large’, ‘great’ (see Middlebrook ). Middleton Baggot and Middleton Priors in Shropshire have early spellings that suggest gem̄thhyll (from gem̄th ‘confluence’ + hyll ‘hill’) + tūn as the origin. This surname is also found in Scotland and Ireland.

History: A Scottish family of this name derives it from lands at Middleto(u)n near Kincardine. The Scottish physician Peter Middleton practiced in New York City after 1752 and was one of the founders of the medical school at King's College (now Columbia University) in 1767. One of the earliest of the Charleston, SC, Middleton family of prominent legislators was Arthur Middleton, born in Charleston in 1681. The name has also been established in Ireland since at least the 13th century; its presence was reinforced by settlers in the 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Melton 7th Lord Lucy was born in 1398, in Derbyshire, England, his father, Thomas Melton, was 31 and his mother, Lady Margaret Clifford, was 31. He married Elizabeth Hilton about 1421, in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died in 1455, in Derbyshire, England, at the age of 57.

Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Hilton около 1421 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1455 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия в возрасте 57 лет.
